message.abstract:
<jats:title>Summary</jats:title>
                <jats:p>
                  There is an urgent need to understand how SARS-CoV-2 infects the airway epithelium and in a subset of individuals leads to severe illness or death. Induced pluripotent stem cells (iPSCs) provide a near limitless supply of human cells that can be differentiated into cell types of interest, including airway epithelium, for disease modeling. We present a human iPSC-derived airway epithelial platform, composed of the major airway epithelial cell types, that is permissive to SARS-CoV-2 infection. Subsets of iPSC-airway cells express the SARS-CoV-2 entry factors
                  <jats:italic>ACE2</jats:italic>
                  and
                  <jats:italic>TMPRSS2.</jats:italic>
                  Multiciliated cells are the primary initial target of SARS-CoV-2 infection. Upon infection with SARS-CoV-2, iPSC-airway cells generate robust interferon and inflammatory responses and treatment with remdesivir or camostat methylate causes a decrease in viral propagation and entry, respectively. In conclusion, iPSC-derived airway cells provide a physiologically relevant
                  <jats:italic>in vitro</jats:italic>
                  model system to interrogate the pathogenesis of, and develop treatment strategies for, COVID-19 pneumonia.
                </jats:p>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Highlights and eTOC blurb</jats:title>
                  <jats:list list-type="bullet">
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>
                        Subsets of human iPSC-airway epithelial cells express SARS-Co-V entry factors
                        <jats:italic>ACE2</jats:italic>
                        and
                        <jats:italic>TMPRSS2</jats:italic>
                        .
                      </jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>iPSC-airway cells are permissive to SARS-CoV-2 infection via multiciliated cells.</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>SARS-CoV-2 infection of iPSC-airway leads to a robust interferon and inflammatory response.</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>iPSC-airway is a physiologically relevant model to study SARS-CoV-2 infection.</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                  </jats:list>
                </jats:sec>
title: Human airway lineages derived from pluripotent stem cells reveal the epithelial responses to SARS-CoV-2 infection
